data.title: Opinion: Bitcoin’s role as a hedge against US tariffs may grow
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Exness financial market strategist Inky Cho said that the recent decline in the cryptocurrency market may be related to the panic of selling caused by Mt.Gox's transfer of BTC to settle debts, and Trump's remarks about the upcoming recession have brought shocks to the stock and cryptocurrency markets. Currently, the correlation between Bitcoin and Nasdaq remains at around 40%, down from the previous peak of 72%, but Bitcoin and ETH and other altcoins are polarizing. Bitcoin is more like a tool to hedge economic uncertainty and current US tariffs, while altcoins including Ethereum are still linked to the Nasdaq market dominated by the technology industry and technology stocks. Therefore, in the long run, Bitcoin's role as a tariff hedging tool may be enhanced, especially when tariffs cause economic instability.

data.title: In the past 4 hours, the entire network has liquidated nearly 45 million US dollars, mainly long orders
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Coinglass data shows that in the past 4 hours, the entire network has been liquidated for $44.9865 million, of which long orders have been liquidated for $42.9277 million and short orders have been liquidated for $2.0588 million.

data.title: 21Shares: Bitcoin is still defending the $84,000-85,000 support area, but long-term positive catalysts are forming
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Matt Mena, 21Shares cryptocurrency research strategist, said that the U.S. stock market has fluctuated amid macro uncertainty, while BTC has shown resilience and is defending the $84,000-85,000 support area. Nic Puckrin, founder of The Coin Bureau, pointed out that the PCE data released on Friday may cause the Federal Reserve to postpone its recent interest rate cut plan, and Bitcoin is not expected to return to its historical highs before the Fed cuts interest rates again.
Long-term positive catalysts for the crypto market are taking shape, one of which is that crypto-friendly Paul Atkins is about to be confirmed as chairman of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, which may unlock Ethereum ETF pledges and approve other digital asset ETFs; in addition, the proposed U.S. strategic Bitcoin reserve legislation is expected to be introduced in May. If formally implemented, Bitcoin will be listed alongside gold as a sovereign-level asset. (benzinga)

data.title: Binance Market Update (2025-03-29)
data.description:
The global cryptocurrency market cap now stands at $2.77T, down by -3.12% over the last day, according to CoinMarketCap data.

Bitcoin (BTC) has been trading between $83,585 and $85,610 over the past 24 hours. As of 09:30 AM (UTC) today, BTC is trading at $83,731, down by -1.86%.

Most major cryptocurrencies by market cap are trading mixed. Market outperformers include FUN, VIDT, and TUT, up by 90%, 21%, and 17%, respectively.

data.title: A whale borrowed 75.69 million DAI with 60,810 ETH as collateral, and the current liquidation price is less than $80
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to monitoring by on-chain analyst Yu Jin, a whale borrowed 75.69 million DAI with 60,810 ETH as collateral, with a liquidation price of US$1,805. The current liquidation price is less than US$80 from the current ETH price.

data.title: Maple CEO brings Wall Street-style institutional lending on-chain with Bitcoin-backed loans
data.description:
Sid Powell, CEO and co-founder of Maple, is bringing an inspired Wall Street lending model to the crypto sphere, using Bitcoin as the basis.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/maple-ceo-pioneers-btc-backed-lending/

data.title: Ethereum Whale Faces Liquidation Risk Amid Price Drop
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, on-chain analyst Yu Jin has reported that a significant Ethereum holder is at risk of liquidation. The whale, holding 67,000 ETH, is just $100 away from the liquidation price of their Ethereum position.

Earlier this month, the whale's position was nearly liquidated due to a drop in ETH prices. However, they managed to avoid liquidation by repaying some of their debt and reducing their position. Following this, a rebound in ETH prices temporarily alleviated the risk of liquidation.

Despite the temporary relief, the whale has not taken any further actions to lower the liquidation price, such as adding more collateral or reducing their position. With ETH prices nearing previous lows, another $100 drop could trigger the liquidation of their holdings.

data.title: The whale holding 67,000 ETH is on the verge of liquidation. The liquidation price of its ETH position is only $100
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of on-chain analyst Yu Jin, the whale holding 67,000 ETH is on the verge of liquidation, and the liquidation price of its ETH position is only $100 away. On March 11, the whale's 67,000 ETH position was on the verge of liquidation due to the decline in ETH prices. He saved his position from liquidation by repaying and reducing his position. Later, the rebound of ETH temporarily freed his position from liquidation risk, and there was no operation to increase margin, reduce positions, etc. to reduce the liquidation price. If ETH falls by another $100, his position will be liquidated.

data.title: Trusted AI project Privasea opens official eSIM reservations today
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, the trusted AI project Privasea has opened official eSIM reservations today. It is reported that this eSIM will be combined with its flagship product ImHuman, and will provide global digital citizens with identity privacy, communication, payment and other integrated services.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Market Sentiment Declines Amid Fear and Greed Index Drop
data.description: According to PANews, the cryptocurrency Fear and Greed Index has significantly decreased to 26, down from 44 the previous day, indicating a shift to a panic level in market sentiment. The index, which ranges from 0 to 100, is calculated based on several factors: volatility (25%), market trading volume (25%), social media trends (15%), market surveys (15%), Bitcoin's dominance in the market (10%), and Google search trends (10%).
